title: Universality of Quantum Gates in Particle and Symmetry Constrained Subspaces
abstract: Simulating physical systems on near-term quantum computers often requires preparing states within constrained subspaces, like those with fixed particle number or spin. We use Lie algebraic techniques to prove that hardware-efficient gates are universal for state preparation in these subspaces. The key mechanism is Pauli $Z$ dressing: commutators of overlapping gates produce Pauli $Z$ operators on shared qubits, acting as spectator projectors that decompose multi-plane rotations into single-plane generators spanning the full $\mathfrak{so}(w)$ algebra, where $w$ is the dimension of the constrained subspace, thereby guaranteeing universality for real state preparation. Adding independent complex phases extends this to $\mathfrak{su}(w)$, enabling arbitrary complex state preparation. We provide a computationally efficient Jacobian criterion for verifying that a circuit can explore any direction on the target manifold from almost any parameter configuration. Our findings are applicable to many problem areas, including Fermi-Hubbard models, Bose-Hubbard models, and molecular electronic structure. We apply our framework to two physical settings: we prove the completeness of the binary encoded multi-level particles ansatz on the conserved-particle-number subspace, and we construct symmetry-preserving circuits for the fuzzy sphere regularisation of the 3D Ising conformal field theory (CFT). For the latter, we variationally prepare the ground and excited states to extract CFT scaling dimensions.
summary: The paper proves that simple, hardware-efficient gates are sufficient to universally prepare any quantum state within a symmetry-constrained subspace. By using commutators to generate Pauli Z projectors, the authors show how local gates can span the full orthogonal and unitary algebras. This provides a robust toolkit for simulating bosonic and fermionic systems on noisy quantum computers.
main_problem: How to design efficient, hardware-efficient quantum gate ansatze that can achieve universal state preparation within symmetry-constrained subspaces (like fixed particle number) without the high circuit depth of non-local operations.
main_result: The authors prove that hardware-efficient multi-plane rotation gates are universal for real state preparation in constrained subspaces via a 'Pauli Z dressing' mechanism, and that adding complex phases extends this to the full SU(w) algebra.
method: Lie algebraic techniques, specifically using the Lie algebra rank condition and commutator calculus to show that overlapping gates generate the necessary generators for the SO(w) and SU(w) groups.
model_or_system: The framework is applied to the Binary Encoded Multi-level Particles Ansatz (BEMPA) for bosonic simulation and a fuzzy sphere regularization of the 3D Ising Conformal Field Theory (CFT) using a 4-qubit electron model.
key_observables: Scaling dimensions of the 3D Ising CFT, energy eigenvalues, and the Jacobian rank for verifying circuit completeness.
important_parameters: Dimension of the subspace (w), number of qubits (Nq), particle number (nq), and the interaction strength/magnetic field in the fuzzy sphere model.
main_assumptions: The proof assumes the availability of continuous-parameter gates and that the circuit can navigate the subspace via a connected graph of bitstrings; it also notes that topological obstructions may still exist globally.
figures_summary: Figures show the decomposition of multi-qubit gates into CNOT and rotation gates, the architecture of a 19-parameter circuit, and convergence plots for VQE/VQD energy extraction.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of constrained state preparation, develops a Lie algebraic framework for universality via Pauli Z dressing, provides a Jacobian criterion for verification, and demonstrates the framework through BEMPA completeness and 3D Ising CFT simulation.
why_interesting: This is highly relevant for many-body dynamics and quantum simulation as it provides a rigorous mathematical foundation for using shallow, symmetry-preserving circuits to simulate complex physical models like Hubbard models and CFTs on near-term hardware.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
